metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=full driver=gap:wait_turn — compressed proxy of a real long agentic session (many tool_call/result pairs per working episode then settle); the 336-event live transcript replays as a clean per-turn working→ready arc with 0 flickers.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## Reasoning

Mistral Vibe is a full agentic coding CLI: each turn streams many tool calls and results before a terminal answer. The real 336-event session at ~/.vibe/logs/session/session_20260706_101952_48e134e3/ is exactly such a workload — 10 user prompts, 168 assistant messages (158 carrying OpenAI-shape `tool_calls[]`), 158 `tool` results, all tool calls auto-agreed (tool_calls_agreed=158, rejected=0, hook_denied=0). It is a clean 10-turn arc with no permission gate stalling any turn.

The adapter's parser (core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/parser.go) implements exactly the no-flicker-within-turn contract this scenario asserts: `user` → `user_message` (opens the turn), `assistant` with non-empty `tool_calls[]` → `assistant_message` with ToolUses (keeps the session WORKING mid-turn, L67-71), `tool` → `tool_result` (L77-81), and a text-only terminal `assistant` → `turn_done` (settles to READY, L72-75). So a turn stays continuously WORKING across every intra-turn assistant-fragment/tool-call/tool-result boundary and settles to READY only on the terminal text message — precisely the acceptance invariant. The scenario's 'at least one tool call' bullet is satisfiable and deterministic here because vibe auto-executes tools (no permission gate), unlike modern claudecode (Sonnet refusing auto-mode) whose recipe had to fall back to plain prompts.

daemon=full: the per-turn ready→working→ready arc, no intra-turn flicker, and never-stuck invariant are all produced from the local messages.jsonl the FilesUnderRoot source tails. Model (mistral-medium-3.5) and context-token count flow from the sibling meta.json sidecar (config.active_model, stats.context_tokens on turn_done). The daemon-lacks list (per-event timestamps, cost, Control, subagent-linking) does not intersect this scenario's spec-required observables — the arc, counts, and final state are all captured — so the gaps are caveats, not a downgrade.

driver=gap:wait_turn: the freshly-scaffolded driver-interactive.sh sets DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; the recipe needs `wait_turn` to block between turns, and that seam is still the stubbed not_implemented SEAM 2 (driver-interactive.sh L168-169). record ports the real turn-done poll from a reference driver before driving.

## Provenance

PROVISIONAL on emission timing only in the general sense; the specific arc is corroborated by the live 336-event transcript replaying cleanly (verified during adapter construction, commit e943e261). A long real engineering session cannot be reproduced deterministically, so the recipe is a compressed proxy: 4 tool-eliciting turns. The no-flicker invariant generalises from the proxy because it is a property of every individual turn.
caveats: Compressed proxy: the recipe runs 4 tool-eliciting turns, not an hours-long session. The no-flicker-within-turn invariant generalises because it is a property of each individual turn (each already interleaves multiple assistant_message/tool_result events before settling).

The vibe JSONL carries no per-event timestamp, cost, or usage; timestamps fall back to tailer.ParseTimestamp (time.Now on live tailing). This does not affect the state arc but means cost is never emitted — the spec asserts cost_nonzero:false.

No Control and no ParentSessionID wiring: irrelevant to this single-session lifecycle scenario, but a subagent-heavy real session would surface child turns as the parent's own working span rather than linked children.

sources.note: ParseLine L49-90: user→user_message, assistant+tool_calls→assistant_message (working, L67-71), text-only assistant→turn_done (settle, L72-75), tool→tool_result — the no-flicker-within-turn contract.
FilesUnderRoot(~/.vibe/logs/session) + JSONLineParser; sessionIDFromPath keys the session off the <id> dir; CommandPattern binds the python vibe proc for liveness. Read-only (no Control).

Real 336-event long agentic session: 10 users, 168 assistant (158 with tool_calls), 158 tool results, 10 text-only turn_done. meta.json: 158 tool_calls_agreed, 0 rejected — auto-executed, no permission gate. Replays as a clean per-turn working→ready arc with 0 flickers.
L113 DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; L168-169 wait_turn is the stubbed not_implemented SEAM 2 → driver=gap:wait_turn.

preconditions: vibe CLI on PATH and authenticated (v2.19.0)
irrlichd is recording (isolated mode or --attach against a --record daemon)
vibe auto-executes tool calls (tool_calls_agreed with no permission gate), so tool-using turns do not stall in waiting
setup: Driver launches vibe in a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/ containing a couple of small files so the list/read prompts have something to touch

script.text: List the files in the current directory using a shell command, then tell me how many there are.
Read the first file you listed and quote its first line.
Run a shell command that prints the word: gamma. Report exactly what it printed.
Summarize in one sentence what you did across the previous three steps.

verify: events.jsonl shows the session entering ready after launch
for EACH of the 4 turns: exactly one ready→working transition, at least one tool_use/tool_result inside the working span for the first three turns, exactly one working→ready at turn end
no state_transition to ready fires inside any turn's working span (no intra-turn flicker between tool-call/result pairs)
no state_transition to waiting fires (vibe auto-executes tools — no permission gate)
